title: The Friendship Behind the War
description: On pages 13–16, this part focuses on the friendship behind the war, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Friendship Behind the War. Vivi’s childhood bond with Koza and the roots of the rebel movement give the civil war a personal face. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: I must confess that I am a little worried.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: About what?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: To be a ruler means having to lay down harsh decisions at times...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I fear Vivi's a little too nice for that.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Igaram...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Yes?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It's fine...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Alabasta is just fine like this...

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: I'll accompany your majesty,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: 'Till the end of my life.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: "Yuba."

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: That's right!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: That's what your dad was talking to my dad about!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: To Toto...?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Yuba's an uninhabited oasis right now,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: So he asked if my dad would be interested in starting up a town there!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: It's situated right in the middle of Western Alabama,

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: So it'll be the perfect intersection for travellers and merchants!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Just imagine how happy people would be if a town were there!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Sounds amazing, right!?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: My dad accepted the offer to start a town there on behalf of the king!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Are you going too?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: 'Course! My dad told me I should just stay here but, well...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: I mean, I know it's gonna be hard work and all,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But I still want to try it! I want to help create a new town with my own two hands!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Yuba will be a vital town for the future of Alabasta!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I'll leave it all in your care, Toto.

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: Thank you, your majesty. I promise the new town will be something to see!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Well, I guess we should say good-bye for now, Vivi.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Please do come visit once the town is set up.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Sure thing, Toto!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Just you wait!

dialogue.speaker: Toto
dialogue.text: I'll build that town so it'll help make this country better than before!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: See ya then, Vivi! In the meanwhile...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You better grow up to be a good princess!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Okay!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Kouza! That's not how to address the princess of our country!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Oww!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: As Kouza said,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'm sure Yuba will help make this country even better than before...

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: ...So what if there hasn't been any rain for the past 3 years!?

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: I still believe in the king! And I'm sure most of the other citizens still do too!

summary: This part develops the friendship behind the war and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
